title: Intersection over Union (IoU)
concept: Intersection over Union (IoU) is a metric that measures the overlap between a predicted bounding box and a ground truth bounding box.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the range of values for normalized bounding box coordinates?
exercises.explain: Normalized coordinates express box values as fractions between 0.0 and 1.0.

cards.title: Overlapping Sticky Notes
cards.body: If you try to place a sticky note directly on top of another one, you might not align them perfectly. How do you measure how close you got? You look at how much they overlap compared to how much space they cover in total.

cards.title: What is IoU?
cards.body: Intersection over Union (IoU) is a mathematical metric used to evaluate bounding box accuracy. It is calculated as the area of the overlapping region (Intersection) divided by the total area covered by both boxes combined (Union).

cards.title: Footprints in the Sand
cards.body: Imagine stepping in someone else's footprint. The area where your boots overlap is the Intersection. The total outline of both footprints in the sand is the Union. The ratio of the overlap to the total area is the IoU.

cards.title: IoU Formula
cards.body: If the overlapping area (Intersection) between a predicted box and a ground truth box is 50 pixels, and their combined area (Union) is 200 pixels, the IoU is 50 / 200 = 0.25.

cards.title: Intersection alone is not enough
cards.body: You might think we only need to look at the intersection (overlap) area. But if a model predicts a massive box covering the entire image, it will have 100% intersection with the object! Dividing by the union penalizes boxes that are too large.

cards.title: Intersection vs. Union
cards.body: Visualizing the numerator and denominator of the IoU metric:

left.text:
The shared overlap area only.
(Area of Overlap)

right.text:
The total combined area.
(Area A + Area B - Overlap)

exercises.prompt: What is the mathematical formula for Intersection over Union (IoU)?
exercises.explain: IoU is calculated by dividing the area of intersection (overlap) by the area of union (total combined area).

exercises.prompt: True or False: If two bounding boxes do not overlap at all, their IoU score is 0.0.
exercises.explain: True. If there is no overlap, the intersection area is 0, which makes the IoU calculation 0 divided by the union, resulting in 0.0.
If a predicted box is too large, the union area becomes huge, which reduces the overall IoU score.
exercises.sentence: Dividing the intersection by the union ensures that boxes that are too ___ are penalized.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following represents a perfect IoU score?
exercises.explain: An IoU score of 1.0 means the predicted box and ground truth box align perfectly, sharing 100% of their area.

exercises.prompt: A model predicts a bounding box for a bicycle. The prediction has an intersection area of 30 square pixels with the ground truth box. The area of their union is 120 square pixels. What is the IoU score?
exercises.explain: Exactly! IoU is Intersection divided by Union, so 30 / 120 = 0.25. Next: let's see how we use this score in practice!
